title: pavonia troyana
intro: Pavonia troyana is a plant species in the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the rosemallow or wild hibiscus. It is native to tropical regions in Central and South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it beneficial for wound healing, skin issues, and gastrointestinal problems. Herbal preparations of Pavonia troyana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions.
